Enter a length in millimeters to see the equivalent value in inches instantly. The tool also supports reverse conversion from inches back to millimeters.

    Tip: For very small parts, such as electronic components or machined pieces, keep at least 3 to 4 decimal places in inches to avoid rounding errors.

    How to convert millimeters to inches

    The millimeter is a metric unit of length, while the inch is part of the imperial system. Their relationship is fixed by international agreement, which defines the inch exactly in terms of the millimeter.

    The core relationship is: 

    1 inch = 25.4 millimeters

    From this, you can build the conversion formulas.

    Formula for mm to inches

    To convert a length from millimeters to inches, divide the millimeter value by 25.4: 

    inches = millimeters / 25.4

    Example: If a part is 32 mm long: 

    inches = 32 / 25.4
inches = 1.2598

    So 32 mm is about 1.2598 inches.

    Formula for inches to mm

    To convert a length from inches to millimeters, multiply the inch value by 25.4: 

    millimeters = inches * 25.4

    Example: If a board is 2.5 inches thick: 

    millimeters = 2.5 * 25.4
millimeters = 63.5

    So 2.5 inches is 63.5 mm.

    Real world example: Converting a drill bit size

    Imagine you are following a woodworking plan written in inches, but your drill bits are labeled in millimeters. The plan calls for a 3/8 inch hole, and you want to know which metric drill bit is closest.

    1. Convert 3/8 inch to a decimal: 3/8 = 0.375 inch.
    2. Convert inches to millimeters using the formula millimeters = inches * 25.4.
    millimeters = 0.375 * 25.4
millimeters = 9.525

    A 9.5 mm drill bit is the closest standard metric size. With the converter, you can quickly check this without doing the math by hand.

    Frequently asked questions about mm to inches

    How do you convert millimeters to inches?
    To convert millimeters to inches, divide the length in millimeters by 25.4. One inch is exactly 25.4 millimeters, so inches = millimeters / 25.4. For example, 50 mm is 50 / 25.4 which is about 1.9685 inches.
    What is 10 mm in inches?
    10 millimeters is about 0.3937 inches. Using the formula inches = millimeters / 25.4, you get 10 / 25.4 which is 0.3937 inches when rounded to four decimal places.
    Why is 1 inch equal to 25.4 millimeters?
    The value 1 inch = 25.4 millimeters comes from an international agreement that defines the inch based on the metric system. Since the millimeter is derived from the meter, this fixed relationship makes it easy to convert between imperial and metric units with high precision.

    Common millimeter to inch conversions

    Here are a few quick reference values that are often used in engineering drawings, 3D printing, and DIY projects:

    • 1 mm = 0.03937 inches
    • 5 mm = 0.19685 inches
    • 10 mm = 0.39370 inches
    • 25 mm = 0.98425 inches (almost 1 inch)
    • 50 mm = 1.96850 inches
    • 100 mm = 3.93701 inches

    For any other value, use the converter above to avoid manual calculation errors and to keep consistent rounding across your project.
